accounting_verdict: QMoE is the only surveyed method clearly publishing a full giant-MoE checkpoint plus all metadata below one BPW (0.807). NanoQuant and LittleBit publish informative whole-model payload/memory figures but not canonical artifact audits.

claim_policy.defensible_novelty_if_achieved: The conjunction of official GLM-5.2 BF16 source, 753B-class autoregressive MoE, complete canonical artifact under 1 BPW, auditable metadata, capability-preserving generation evaluation, and measured Apple-local execution—not sub-1-bit compression by itself.
safe: Gravity targets a fully auditable canonical-artifact rate below 1 BPW for official GLM-5.2 BF16 weights.

QMoE is the closest prior giant-MoE baseline and already demonstrates a fully metadata-accounted 0.807-BPW checkpoint; Gravity evaluates a materially different autoregressive GLM architecture and capability regime.
NanoQuant and LittleBit establish dense low-rank binary references under different training budgets, scales, hardware, and denominators.
External results are contextual baselines, not a cross-paper leaderboard.
unsafe: First fully accounted giant-MoE sub-1-bit checkpoint.
Better than QMoE, NanoQuant, or LittleBit without matched model, tokenizer, harness, denominator, and hardware.
Treating BitNet 1.58, STBLLM 0.55, BTC index rate, or quantized-matrix BPW as complete physical artifact BPW.

methods.evaluation: broad peer-size general, math, code, instruction and dialogue tests; no floating-teacher preservation contract
methods.glm_comparability: architecture context only: dense 2.4B native pretraining, not GLM PTQ
methods.method_class: native ternary architecture trained from scratch

rate.physical_boundary: GPU format packs four trits per byte: 2 physical bits per linear weight before higher-precision embeddings/other tensors

methods.runtime: CUDA kernels and bitnet.cpp CPU runtime
methods.scope: dense 0.7B-3.9B experiments; released 2.4B; ternary linear weights and per-token INT8 activations

structured_comparison.source_or_teacher_precision: none: native ternary training from scratch
structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: ternary linear weights plus per-token INT8 activations

methods.training: 2B4T uses 4T-token pretraining plus SFT/DPO; not post-training compression

methods.evaluation: WikiText-2 and eight zero-shot commonsense tasks
methods.glm_comparability: useful below-3-bit representation evidence; not PTQ, MoE, complete storage, or GLM scale
methods.method_class: QAT initialized from floating pretrained checkpoints

rate.physical_boundary: effective model size includes embeddings but not complete metadata/container accounting; ternary generally requires 2 physical bits

methods.reproducibility: public code; paper results used internal Meta infrastructure
methods.scope: dense MobileLLM 125M-1.5B and Llama-3 1/3/8B; embeddings/output stay floating

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: quantized decoder weights; embeddings/output remain floating

methods.training: up to 30B QAT tokens at <=2 bits on 16 GPUs

methods.method_class: training-free Hessian-aware weight-only PTQ with incoherence and LDLQ

rate.physical_boundary: transforms/scales/unquantized tensors/packaging are not fully accounted

methods.runtime: OPT-66B A6000 about 81 ms/token versus 53 ms for paper's OPTQ comparison

methods.evaluation: perplexity, five zero-shot tasks, limited chat generations
methods.glm_comparability: closest established extreme lattice/VQ family with a MoE appendix, but only 2-bit and incomplete accounting
methods.method_class: Hadamard incoherence plus E8 lattice codebooks

rate.physical_boundary: codebook/sign overhead <0.01 BPW per quantized matrix, but lm-head/norms/floating tensors excluded

methods.scope: dense Llama 7B-70B; appendix Falcon-180B and no-FT Mixtral-8x7B
methods.training: main results use calibration fine-tuning; no-FT variant exists

methods.evaluation: perplexity, zero-shot tasks, MMLU and GSM8K appendix
methods.glm_comparability: MoE/PTQ relevant, but not sub-1-bit and far smaller

rate.physical_boundary: codes, FP16 codebooks and scales for quantized matrices only; floating parameters/artifact overhead excluded

methods.runtime: 70B quantization 10-14 days on one A100 or 3-4 days on eight; NVIDIA/CPU inference kernels
methods.scope: dense Llama-2 7/13/70B, Mistral-7B, Mixtral-8x7B; Mixtral gate unquantized

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: additive-codebook weights; Mixtral router floating; activations floating

methods.training: beam/block refinement; optional 4M-16M-token full-model KL distillation

methods.glm_comparability: dense >=2-bit paper evidence; repo-scale claims are unmatched
methods.method_class: second-order vector PTQ with residual/outlier codebooks

rate.physical_boundary: Transformer-block indices/codebooks/scales only; embeddings/head/nonlinear tensors/packaging excluded

methods.reproducibility: public CUDA/Triton tools; community models carry a caveat
methods.runtime: four A100s: about 2 h at 7B and 19 h at 70B
methods.scope: peer-reviewed dense Llama-2/3 and Mistral through 70B; later repo scale claims are not equivalent paper evidence

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: vector-quantized Transformer block weights; activations floating

methods.method_class: Hessian-aware vector PTQ with EM codebooks

rate.physical_boundary: formula includes index/quantized-codebook overhead but not a complete model denominator or artifact

methods.reproducibility: algorithm public; headline mobile engine not fully released
methods.runtime: one H100 roughly 3-11 h for 70B; Snapdragon proof on Llama-3-8B

methods.evaluation: perplexity, seven zero-shot tasks, dialogue examples
methods.glm_comparability: warning that 1-bit arithmetic is not 1 physical BPW; dense only

rate.physical_boundary: headline is arithmetic weight rate; checkpoint ratios imply 1.5-1.9, while NanoQuant complete-decode re-accounting gives ~2.88 due to scales/means/bitmaps

methods.runtime: no optimized end-to-end binary inference kernel

methods.evaluation: perplexity and seven zero-shot tasks; no strong free-form suite
methods.glm_comparability: not MoE and not physically sub-1-bit

rate.physical_boundary: own 2:4 kernel stores six bits per four original weights before scales/group metadata

methods.runtime: NVIDIA sparse tensor-core layer benchmark; not end-to-end generation

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: N:M sparse binary weights; activations floating

methods.evaluation: C4 masked-LM validation loss plus Arxiv/GitHub/StackExchange/Wikipedia losses; no modern autoregressive capability suite
methods.glm_comparability: critical closest prior: giant BF16-source MoE, complete sub-1 artifact and direct runtime; materially different encoder-decoder MLM, unusual zero-heavy experts, and shallow capability evidence
methods.method_class: retraining-free GPTQ-style expert PTQ plus lossless dictionary/entropy encoding

rate.physical_boundary: paper states full model plus all metadata; expert natural sparsity 88.6%

methods.reproducibility: public compressed checkpoints, code and CUDA kernel
methods.runtime: direct compressed inference on 4xA6000 or 8xRTX3090, under 5% over idealized BF16 lower bound
methods.scope: official BF16 SwitchTransformer base/large-128 and c2048 1.6T; ternary experts, BF16 non-experts

sources.title: QMoE: Sub-1-Bit Compression of Trillion-Parameter Models

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: ternary experts; BF16 non-experts; activations floating

methods.training: post-training; one A6000, about 16 h for c2048

methods.glm_comparability: peer-reviewed sub-1 claim but accounting/reproducibility insufficient for physical comparison
methods.method_class: binary-codebook PTQ with learnable invertible transform

rate.physical_boundary: formula omits required row scales/biases and floating embeddings/head; reported memory is not an audited artifact

methods.reproducibility: code repository at cutoff contains only a nine-byte README; no algorithm/kernels/checkpoints
methods.runtime: preliminary H800 layer kernel, not a matched end-to-end runtime

methods.evaluation: perplexity, six zero-shot tasks and limited qualitative generation
methods.glm_comparability: strongest dense PTQ comparator; dense 70B, gradient-calibrated, CUDA-only and not artifact-audited
methods.method_class: low-rank binary-factor PTQ with ADMM and scale-only KL distillation

rate.physical_boundary: nominal rate covers decoder-block linear factors plus FP16 row/column scales; whole-model number is memory/payload, not canonical file audit

methods.reproducibility: open CUDA quantization/GEMV/GEMM code; no official prequantized collection identified
methods.runtime: one H100, about 13 h at 70B; RTX3050 20.11 tok/s and 5.86 GB peak for nominal 0.55
methods.scope: dense Llama/Qwen/Gemma/Rnj-1 0.6B-70B; no MoE support listed

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: binary-factor weights and FP16 scales; activations floating

methods.glm_comparability: important sub-1 QAT representation reference; not one-pass PTQ, MoE, or GLM scale

rate.physical_boundary: nominal covers Transformer factors/scales; 70B figures are estimates and 70B QAT was not run

methods.runtime: A100 custom kernel; Llama-2-7B nominal 0.1 reports 203.2 tok/s versus 82.6 FP16
methods.scope: dense OPT/Llama/Phi/QwQ evaluated through 32B; embeddings/lm-head FP16

structured_comparison.weight_activation_scope: binary-factor decoder weights; FP16 embeddings/head; activations floating

rate_taxonomy.canonical_artifact_bpw: Actual checkpoint bytes including all tensors, scales, codebooks, bitmaps, offsets, padding, headers, manifest and configuration.
rate_taxonomy.decoded_tensor_payload_bpw: All tensors required to reconstruct or infer, divided by original logical weights.
rate_taxonomy.nominal_or_method_bpw: Index/arithmetic/quantized-matrix rate claimed by the method.
rate_taxonomy.ranking_rule: Rates from different taxonomy levels are not ranked as equal evidence.

source_policy: Primary paper PDFs are content-hashed; official code repositories are commit-pinned. No secondary result is used for a method row.
